title: Market Brief — Monday, April 6, 2026
summary: Compute is in severe shortage on legacy silicon and loosening fast on next-gen. H100 availability sits at 10% (69/287), H200 at a critical 5% (95/378), while B200 shows 45% oversupply (56/291) after a massive 49% price crash from $7.49 to $3.81/hr in a single day. The H100 spot spike of +27% signals desperate demand for proven chips even as Blackwell inventory piles up unsold.
paragraphs: Compute is in severe shortage on legacy silicon and loosening fast on next-gen. H100 availability sits at 10% (69/287), H200 at a critical 5% (95/378), while B200 shows 45% oversupply (56/291) after a massive 49% price crash from $7.49 to $3.81/hr in a single day. The H100 spot spike of +27% signals desperate demand for proven chips even as Blackwell inventory piles up unsold.

At 1.25x TCO, H100 true inference cost is $7.45/M tokens and H200 is $5.56/M. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M output is burning cash catastrophically, selling at an estimated loss of $5 or more per million tokens on H100-class hardware. Meta Llama 4 Maverick at $0.60/M is in the same boat. OpenAI o3 Pro at $80/M output prints money at 96%+ margins. Grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

B200 at $3.81/hr spot versus $4.11 OCPI with 96% annualized volatility and 45% availability is overvalued and due for further correction as utilization craters at 54.8%. H200 at $2.16 spot with only 5% availability and 94.6% utilization is the scarce asset to accumulate before pricing catches up to demand.
bullets: 5% of H200 GPUs are available, the tightest supply across all classes with only 95 of 378 units free.
45% of B200 inventory sits idle as utilization dropped 27.9 percentage points in 30 days.
$3.81 median B200 spot is down 49.1% day-over-day, the largest single-day GPU price collapse in this dataset.
21.41 LLMflation index means token prices have fallen roughly 79% since GPT-4 launch at base 100.
$0.38 per million output tokens makes DeepSeek V3.2 the cheapest model tracked, priced well below production cost.
94.6% H200 utilization surged 28.6 percentage points in 30 days, signaling accelerating demand for high-bandwidth memory inference.
